query: Meta AI Instagram teen self-harm suicide parental guardian alerts feature rollout

mako.raw:
{"query":"Meta AI Instagram teen self-harm suicide parental guardian alerts feature rollout","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1884,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://about.fb.com/news/2026/07/keeping-parents-informed-teens-distress-conversations-meta-ai/","title":"Alerting Parents if Teens Show Signs of Distress in Conversations With Meta AI","content":"2 days ago — Parents using Instagram supervision tools will now be notified if their teen discusses suicide or self-harm with Meta AI.
We’ll now let parents using Instagram supervision tools know if their teen discusses suicide or self-harm with Meta AI, and will provide expert resources to help parents approach these conversations with their teen.
Now, we’ll also proactively alert supervising parents if their teen’s Meta AI chat suggests they may be at risk, based on signals developed with experts.
These alerts are live now for parents using Instagram parental supervision in the US, UK, Australia and Canada, and will be available for supervising parents globally by the end of the year.
In addition to ongoing consultation with our AI Wellbeing Expert Council, Suicide and Self-Harm Advisory Group, and Youth Advisors, we sought feedback from over 75 mental health clinicians on how Meta AI responds to teens’ prompts about suicide and self-harm.
In October, we announced Limited Content as a stricter content setting for parents who prefer a more restrictive experience for their teens on Instagram — and now that stricter setting will apply to AI experiences too."},{"url":"https://abcnews.com/GMA/Family/meta-alert-parents-teen-ai-chats-suggest-suicide/story?id=134794158","title":"Meta says it will alert parents if teen AI chats suggest suicide risk - ABC News","content":"1 day ago — New Meta AI safety tools include parent alerts, expanded protections and emergency response features for teens.
Meta is introducing new safety measures it says are designed to better protect teens using its AI tools, including a feature that it says will alerts parents if the company believes a teen's conversation with Meta AI suggests they may be considering suicide or self-harm.
The new "Parent Alerts" feature will begin rolling out Thursday to parents using Instagram's parental supervision tools in the United States, the United Kingdom, Australia and Canada.
Meta said the effort builds on the more than 19,000 emergency referrals it made globally across Facebook and Instagram last year.
If parents enable the stricter content setting for their teen through Instagram's parental supervision tools, the teen's interactions with Meta AI will also be more limited."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/tech/meta-ai-teen-suicide-self-harm-parent-alert","title":"Meta AI suicide discussions with teens will trigger parental alert | Mashable","content":"7 hours ago — A child looks at a phone screen. Teen conversations about suicide with Meta AI will trigger parent notification. Credit: Viva Tung/Mashable/ ...Read more
"Meta's claim that it will alert supervising parents if their teen's AI chat suggests they may be at risk of self-harm is a step in the right direction, but the announcement should be greeted with skepticism," Brendan Bouffard, staff attorney for the children's advocacy nonprofit Fairplay, told Mashable in a statement.
Bouffard said Fairplay's 2025 research on Meta's Teen Accounts found ineffective or nonexistent safety tools.
In February, Meta launched a similar suicide and self-harm notification feature for Instagram.
Meta also announced Thursday the addition of a limited content setting for Meta AI.
If you don't like the phone, consider using the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline Chat."},{"url":"https://fairplayforkids.org/statement-on-meta-ai-teen-self-harm-alerts-to-supervising-parents/","title":"Statement on Meta AI Teen Self-Harm Alerts to Supervising ...","date":"1 day ago","content":"1 day ago — If Meta truly wanted to reduce suicide risk among young people, it would stop designing Meta AI and its character chatbots to maximize ...Read more"}]}

Meta has announced a new safety feature: if a teen brings up suicide or self-harm while chatting with Meta AI, parents will now get notified. The company shared the update in a blog post on Thursday, calling it an expansion of the parental tools it already offers. This isn’t a standalone feature; it adds to the parental supervision system Meta has already rolled out, which lets parents keep an eye on how their teen uses Meta AI. For now, only parents who have Instagram’s supervision controls turned on will get these alerts, though Meta plans to bring the same alerts to its other apps over time. New Meta AI tools alert parents to possible teen suicide or self-harm risk. Meta is introducing new safety measures it says are designed to better protect teens using its AI tools, including a feature that it says will alerts parents if the company believes a teen's conversation with Meta AI suggests they may be considering suicide or self-harm. If an alert is sent, Meta said parents will also receive expert-backed resources to help them navigate conversations about suicide and self-harm with their teens.
